questions.question_text: The energy transformation that takes place in a solar cell is
questions.explanation: Light energy is converted into electrical energy.

questions.question_text: All flowering plants end their life cycle with
questions.explanation:
A flowering plant is simply a plant that produces flowers, and these flowers turn into fruits.

Flowering plants go through several stages: Germination, Pollination, Fertilization, and Dispersal.

The final stage in a flowering plant life cycle is seed dispersal .

questions.question_text: Soil texture is a term used to describe the

options.A: amount of water soil can hold.
options.B: arrangement of soil particles in a given soil.
options.C: properties of soil, silt and clay in a sample of soil.

questions.explanation: Soil texture (such as loam, sandy loam or clay) refers to the proportion of sand, silt and clay sized particles that make up the mineral fraction of the soil.

questions.question_text: Which of the following processes is an example of a physical change?
questions.explanation:
Physical changes: 

The substance involved in a physical change is structurally identical before and after the change. Texture, shape, temperature, and a change in the state of matter are examples of physical changes.

Magnetization of iron is a physical change as it is a temporary change.

questions.question_text: An example of a storage pest is
questions.explanation:
A storage pest is an insect or other animal that damages or destroys stored food or other stored valuable organic matter.

Grain borers, weevils and Angoumois grain moths are able to feed on whole, healthy grains, they are considered storage pests

questions.question_text: The solid component found on the filter paper and the liquid component that flows into a container during filtration are respectively called
questions.explanation: The solid that remains on the filter paper is called the residue , and the liquid that passes through the filter paper is called the filtrate

questions.question_text: The ion that causes hardness in water is
questions.explanation: Calcium (Ca 2+ ) and magnesium (Mg 2+ ) are the causes the hardness of water.

questions.question_text: The main reason for staking tomato is to ensure

options.D: that fruits do not get rotten.

questions.explanation:
Staking tomatoes provides support to help keep plants off the ground while assisting in their upward growth habit.

Because many diseases and insects start at the ground level, using a structured system to keep them away from ground contact is prudent.

Staking can also be used to prevent the fruit

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about clayey soil is not true? It

options.A: swells when dry and shrinks when wet.
options.C: is difficult to plough when dry

questions.explanation: Clayey soil rather swells when wet and shrinks when dry

questions.question_text: It is not advisable to use old electrical gadgets because they
questions.explanation: Old devices use much more energy than new models

questions.question_text: Which of the following planets are closer to the sun than the Earth?
questions.explanation: Venus and Mercury are more closer to the sun, Mars & Saturn are far away from the sun but more closer to the Earth

questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements about pressure in liquids is/are correct ?

I. Pressure in liquids at the same level acts equally in all directions.

II. Pressure in liquids depend on the area of the liquid.

III. Pressure in liquids decreases with depth.
questions.explanation:
Pascal's law states that pressure in liquids is transmitted equally in all directions.

Pressure in liquids does not depend on the surface area.

The pressure exerted by a liquid increases with depth.

questions.question_text: Soil depletion is not caused by
questions.explanation: Afforestation is the process of introducing trees and tree seedlings to an area that has previously not been forested. Afforestation can be done through tree planting and seeding, naturally or artificially.

questions.question_text: What force makes a ripe mango fall from a tree?
questions.explanation:
Gravitational force 

The force due to which any matter is attracted towards the centre of the Earth is known as the force of gravity . The mango fell due to the force of gravity of Earth.

questions.question_text: Urea is a good source of
questions.explanation: Urea and fertilizers containing urea are excellent sources of nitrogen for crop production.

questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements are reasons for conserving energy?

I. Insufficient production of energy

II. Increasing demand of energy

III. Limiting production of carbon (IV) oxide
questions.explanation: NOTE: One must still conserve energy even when demand of energy is not high. Conversation will ensure that the resources used for the generation of the energy is reserved for future generations

questions.question_text:
Which of the following bodies are natural sources of light?

I. Moon

II. Sun

III. Glow Worm
questions.explanation:
A natural source of light is light that occurs naturally without the involvement of humans.

Natural sources of light can come from either an object or a living species.

Examples include the Sun , stars, lightning, jellyfish, fireflies, glowworms , angler fish, etc.

The moon is not a natur

questions.question_text:
Which of the following characteristics is/are of the image formed in a pinhole camera? The image is

I. inverted.

II. virtual.

III. diminished.
questions.explanation:
Characteristics of image formed in a pinhole camera 

(i) The image formed is real because it is visible on the screen

(ii) It is inverted 

(iii) It is generally smaller/diminished than the size of the object

questions.question_text: The floor of a room has length 20 m and breadth 15 m. Determine the area of the floor.
questions.explanation:
Area of a rectangle = Length x Breadth

Area of the floor = 20 m x 15 m = 300 m 2

questions.question_text: Rainfall is a result of
questions.explanation:
When the pressure is low, the air is free to rise into the atmosphere where it cools and condenses.

This condensation forms clouds made of water droplets and ice crystals around dust particles in the sky. Eventually the water vapor in the clouds condenses and falls as rain.

questions.question_text: An example of a first class lever is
questions.explanation:
First Class Lever 

A first-class lever has the fulcrum/pivot in the middle and the load and effort on each side of the fulcrum.

 Examples of a first class lever 

Pliers, scissors, a crow bar, a claw hammer, a see-saw and a weighing balance.

questions.question_text: Green plants have the ability to make their own food hence they are known as
questions.explanation: Green plants are called producers because they can prepare their own food by a process called photosynthesis.

questions.question_text: The periodic table is an arrangement of elements according to increasing
questions.explanation: In periodic table, the elements are listed in order of increasing atomic number . The atomic number is the number of protons in the nucleus of an atom.

questions.question_text: The ovary, style and stigma are collectively known as the
An atom 23 11 X forms bond by losing one electron, the charge on the ion formed is
questions.explanation:
When an atom loses an electron it has positive charge (Cation) and gain negative charge (Anion)

Since the atomic number is 11, the atom in question is Sodium (Na). Sodium has +1 charge (Na +1 = Na + ) when it loses an electron during the bond.

questions.question_text: The hard outer surface of a tooth is called
questions.explanation:
Root 

The root is the part of the tooth that extends into the bone and holds the tooth in place. It makes up approximately two-thirds of the tooth.

 Enamel 

The enamel is the outermost layer of a tooth. As the hardest tissue in your body, it helps to protect teeth from bacteria. It also provides

questions.question_text: People who travel in space are called
questions.explanation:
Geologist : scientist who study the Earth: its history, nature, materials and processes.

 Astronaut : a person who has been trained for travelling in space

questions.question_text: The practice of growing cowpea and maize on a piece of land at the same time is known as
questions.explanation:
Mixed Cropping 

When two or more crops are grown on the same land simultaneously (at the same time), it is known as mixed cropping

 Mixed Farming 

Growing crops as well as livestock , animal husbandry,etc all on same farm or land.

questions.question_text: Which of the following instruments can be used to measure the diameter of a circle?
questions.explanation:
Metre-rule is used to measure length. Diameter of a circle is a length.

 Thermometer is used to measure temperature

 Ammeter is used for measuring either direct (DC) or alternating (AC) electric current, in amperes.

questions.question_text: The shoot system of a flowering plant develops from the
questions.explanation: A shoot or seedling originates from the plumule of the seed’s embryo.

questions.question_text:
The diagram below is an illustration of a transistor.

The arrow shows the direction of:
questions.explanation: The arrow in the symbol indicates the direction of flow of conventional current (electron movement)

questions.question_text: Digestion of protein begins in the stomach and ends in the
questions.explanation: Mechanical digestion of protein begins in the mouth and continues in the stomach and small intestine . Chemical digestion of protein begins in the stomach and ends in the small intestine .

questions.question_text: Which of the following substances is a compound?
questions.explanation:
Copper and sulphur are elements. Diamond is made up of only carbon elements so can't be classified as a compound because compounds are made up of two or more different elements.
